rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC. DBA GREAT VS. NEIL ALLEN BLANCHARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Assignee David Mckim'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Post-Judgment Interrogatories (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 4, 2015, Line 1, Assignee David Mckim's Motion To Compel Further Response To Post-Judgment Interrogatories (Duplicate entry - For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only). (Part 2 of 2.) But McKim then contradicts himself, stating in the next sentence that it is not satisfied with CTA's response, calling it "bad faith gamesmanship" and then in the last sentence of the Reply demands that CTA respond to Interrogatory 1 in "full" and "without objection." CTA briefly complains in its "Limited Opposition" that it should not be compelled to provide the information requested insofar as "this would require hours of clerical or professional time to review each file." But CTA fails to offer a declaration in support of this contention, and does not quantify whether "hours" of time means two hours or twenty hours. In the absence of facts submitted in declaration with more specificity, the Court cannot presume and does not know that responding to Interrogatory 1 would be unduly burdensome. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC. DBA GREAT VS. NEIL ALLEN BLANCHARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motin And Motion To Compel Further Response To Post-Judgment Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 4, 2015, Line 1, ASSIGNEE DAVID MCKIM's Motion To Compel Further Response To Post-Judgment Interrogatories. (Part 1 of 2.) Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Movant David M. McKim ("McKim's") Motion to Compel Further Response to Post Judgment Interrogatories is GRANTED. Since McKim failed to seek sanctions, failed to signify a request for sanctions in caption, and failed to offer proof in declaration of time spent, rates charged, or costs incurred, sanctions as against all Parties are DENIED. McKim shall prepare a form of the order consistent with this tentative ruling and shall bring a copy of the proposed order to the hearing, if any. In the absence of a hearing, the prevailing party shall timely comply with Cal. R. Ct. 3.1312. Plaintiff Creditors Trade Association, Inc.'s ("CTA") submits a Limited Opposition to Defendant's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Post-Judgment Interrogatory, in which it agrees to comply, in part, with Interrogatory No. 1, with the exception that it "continues to object to the defendant's request to provide the attorney of record, if any, for each defendant and the prayer of the complaint." Notably, CTA fails to submit responses in a Separate Statement, which can be fatal, in and of itself. See Cal. R. Ct. Rule 3.1345; Mills v. U.S. Bank, 166 Cal. App. 4th 871, 893 (2008). In Reply to CTA's partial capitulation, McKim seems satisfied, "request[ing] a thirty day postponement" of the instant Motion "to see if [CTA] indeed follows through on its 'agreement.'" (Continued to Part 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE M. CRITSER et al VS. HEWLETT-PACKARD COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 4, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT SIMPLO TECHNOLOGY USA LOGISTIC COMPANY, LTD'S Motion To Transfer Venue. Denied. CCP § 397(c) provides discretionary power to the court to transfer a case "when the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice would be promoted by the change." The court should only consider the convenience of nonparty witnesses. (Wrin v. Ohlandt (1931) 213 Cal.158, 160.) The declarations from the nonparty witnesses must "set forth the names of the witnesses, the nature of the testimony expected from each and the reasons why attendance upon the trial would be inconvenient." (Edwards v. Pierson (1957) 156 Cal.App.2d 72, 75.) The declarations submitted by Defendant do not set forth "the nature of the testimony expected from each." (Id.) Rather, each of the five declarants state in a conclusory fashion that they responded to the incident and prepared a report of their findings. (Declaration of Nicolas Martin, Exs. I-N.) There is no information provided as to what these declarants will testify about and therefore, the Court cannot determine whether their testimony will be necessary. (See Edwards v. Pierson (1957) 156 Cal.App.2d 72, 75, "the object of the stated requirement of the affidavit is to enable the court to determine the materiality of the evidence or the necessity of testimony from such witnesses.") Because Defendant did not meet its evidentiary burden, the motion is denied. The Court reminds Plaintiffs to use exhibit tabs in any future motions, as required by CRC 3.1110(f). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or Alternatively Issue And Evidentiary Sanctions With Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 4, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT ALYCE CRAFT's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or Alternatively Issue And Evidentiary Sanctions With Request For Monetary Sanctions. (Part 1 of 2.) Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: All Parties to appear, March 4, 2015, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 301. Craft's motion for terminating sanctions is GRANTED. Craft's request for monetary sanctions is GRANTED. Using contact information available, movant shall notify opposing party of this tentative and of the date, time, and location of the instant hearing. At hearing, Movant shall report to the Court efforts made to inform opposing party of this hearing. Craft shall prepare a proposed order integrating this tentative. Procedural History. On February 27, 2015, Alyce D. Craft's ("Craft") Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or Alternatively Issue And Evidentiary Sanctions With Request For Monetary Sanctions came on regularly for hearing. The Motion was properly filed and served. Plaintiffs BRUCE LEE CEARLOCK, and NILA CEARLOCK ("Cearlock"), however, failed to oppose the Motion. On February 26, 2015, the Court issued a tentative ruling ordering all parties to appear at the Hearing set for February 27, 2015. But Cearlock, failed to contest the tentative ruling and failed to appear at hearing. In failing to appear at the hearing, Cearlock has stipulated to the authority of the Judge Pro Tem. Prior Hearing. At hearing on February 27, 2015, counsel for Craft presented competent evidence and persuasive argument for why terminating sanctions would be proper in this case. Good cause appeared for granting Craft's motion for terminating sanctions. (Continued to Part 2.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CRAIG HOLLIS VS. UNIVERSAL PROTECTION SERVICE GP, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alt, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 4, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANTS UNIVERSAL PROTECTION SERVICE GP, INC AND CHRISTIAN AGARDI'S Motion for Summary Judgment Or, In the Alternative, Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. The motion is denied as to the discrimination, retaliation, and wrongful discharge in violation of public policy causes of action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant constructively discharged plaintiff. See Turner v. Anheuser Busch (1994) 7 Cal.4th 1238, 1244-1245; Plaintiff's deposition, 67:12-68:6). The trier of fact will decide whether the working conditions were so intolerable that a reasonable person in plaintiff's position would feel compelled to resign or whether plaintiff would have left the job notwithstanding the misconduct. Defendant is basically raising a factual issue regarding causation. The motion is denied as to the harassment and failure to correct harassment causes of action. The court looks at the totality of the circumstances to determine harassment. See Miller v. Dept. of Corrections (2005) 36 Cal.4th 446, 462. Whether defendant took immediate and appropriate corrective action is a factual issue. A trier of fact could find that defendant's investigations were half-hearted and defendant should have installed the video camera prior to the fifth graffiti incident. The trier of fact could also find that Mr. Agardi's conduct constitutes harassment. The motion is denied as to the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: THE REAL REAL, INC,A DELAWARE COPORATION VS. FASHION FLASH, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: The Realreal Inc.S Omnibus Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 4, 2015, Line 5, PLAINTIFF THE REALREAL, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests. Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Real Real Inc.'s ("Real Real") Omnibus Motion to Compel Further Responses to Discovery Requests is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. The Real Real's Written Objections to Evidence in Opposition to its Omnibus Motion to Compel Further Responses is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. The Real Real's request for sanctions is held in abeyance pending the outcome of the hearing. All Parties shall appear on March 4, 2015, at 10:00 a.m. in Department 301. Appearance via court-call is permissible. At the Hearing the Parties shall submit to the Court an Amended Separate Statement integrating Defendants amended responses. The Amended Separate Statement shall comply with Rule of Court 3.1345, with the following exceptions: (1) the contents of the statement shall be in the form of a multi-column chart in the model of a motion for summary judgment, see Cal. R. Ct. 3.1350(h); (2) double sided, (3) single spaced, and (4) in ten point font. No later than noon on Monday March 2, 2015, Defendants shall e-mail an electronic copy (in Microsoft Word format) of its current Separate Statement, and all supplemental discovery responses. No later than noon on Tuesday, March 3, 2015, the Real Real shall e-mail back to Defendants an electronic copy (in Microsoft Word Format) of its Amended Separate Statement in the format set out above. Prior to the Hearing, on March 4, 2015, Defendants shall serve its Amended Separate Statement on the Real Real either via e-mail or in hard copy format. Keep in mind, the Court would prefer brevity of argument in these documents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 4, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT LEGAL AID SOCIETY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Continued to March 25, 2015 to give moving and opposing parties the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers and opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than March 4, 2015 at 4:00 p.m. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the reply papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KWIK BOND POLYMERS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIAB VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 4, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Defendant is a public entity and therefore any claims against it must be based upon statute imposing liability. Plaintiff has not alleged any statutory basis for its claims for quantum meruit, negligence, or negligence per se. Plaintiff has leave to amend to allege, if it can in good faith, a statutory basis for liability against Defendant. Moreover, Civil Code section 9550 explicitly states that it does not apply to state entities, and while not pled directly in the First Amended Complaint, the Court takes judicial notice of the fact that, as a department of the State of California, Defendant is a state entity and therefore section 9550 does not apply here. (CC § 9550(e).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
